## [​](https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/quickstart\#get-your-free-tavily-api-key)  Get your free Tavily API key

Head to the [Tavily Platform](https://app.tavily.com/) and sign in (or create an account). Then, copy one of your API keys from your dashboard.

[**Get your free API key** \\
\\
You get 1,000 free API Credits every month. **No credit card required.**](https://app.tavily.com/)

## [​](https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/quickstart\#install-tavily)  Install Tavily

Install the Tavily SDK in your language of choice.

Python

JavaScript

```
pip install tavily-python
```

## [​](https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/quickstart\#start-searching-with-tavily)  Start searching with Tavily

Run your first Tavily Search in 4 lines of code. Simply replace the API key in this snippet with your own.

Python

JavaScript

cURL

```
from tavily import TavilyClient

tavily_client = TavilyClient(api_key="tvly-YOUR_API_KEY")
response = tavily_client.search("Who is Leo Messi?")

print(response)
```

## [​](https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/quickstart\#next-steps)  Next steps

That’s all it takes to start using Tavily’s basic features!If you want to learn how to implement more complex workflows in Python, check out our intermediate-level [Getting Started notebook](https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1dWGtS3u4ocCLebuaa8Ivz7BkZ_40IgH1).Or, dive deep into our API and read about the different parameters on our [API Reference](https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/api-reference/introduction) page, and learn how to integrate natively with one of our [SDKs](https://docs.tavily.com/sdk).
